q: It was raining. The match was cancelled. (due to)
a: The match was cancelled due to the rain.

q: The players trained hard. They won the championship. (so)
a: The players trained hard, so they won the championship.

q: She failed the test. She hadn't studied. (because)
a: She failed the test because she hadn't studied.

q: The power went out. We couldn't finish the movie. (as a result)
a: The power went out; as a result, we couldn't finish the movie.

q: He didn't bring his ID. He wasn't allowed to enter. (since)
a: Since he didn't bring his ID, he wasn't allowed to enter.

q: The fire alarm rang. Everyone left the building. (so)
a: The fire alarm rang, so everyone left the building.

q: She didn't sleep well. She looked exhausted. (therefore)
a: She didn't sleep well; therefore, she looked exhausted.

q: There was a storm. The power lines were damaged. (as a consequence)
a: There was a storm; as a consequence, the power lines were damaged.

q: He was late. The traffic was terrible. (due to)
a: He was late due to the terrible traffic.

q: We practiced a lot. We performed confidently. (as a result)
a: We practiced a lot; as a result, we performed confidently.

q: The road was icy. Several accidents occurred. (because)
a: Several accidents occurred because the road was icy.

q: She ate too much sugar. She got a toothache. (as a result of)
a: She got a toothache as a result of eating too much sugar.

q: They were careless. The project failed. (as a consequence of)
a: The project failed as a consequence of their carelessness.

q: The teacher gave clear instructions. The students understood everything. (so)
a: The teacher gave clear instructions, so the students understood everything.

q: He didn't revise the draft. It had many errors. (because)
a: It had many errors because he didn't revise the draft.

q: I spilled water on my notes. I had to rewrite everything. (therefore)
a: I spilled water on my notes; therefore, I had to rewrite everything.

q: They were arguing loudly. The neighbours called the police. (as a consequence)
a: They were arguing loudly; as a consequence, the neighbours called the police.

q: She was very polite. Everyone liked her. (as a result)
a: She was very polite, as a result of which everyone liked her.

q: The weather was bad. The plane couldn't land. (due to)
a: The plane couldn't land due to the bad weather.

q: He followed the recipe exactly. The cake turned out perfectly. (so)
a: He followed the recipe exactly, so the cake turned out perfectly.

q: The instructions were unclear. We made many mistakes. (because)
a: We made many mistakes because the instructions were unclear.

q: She trained every morning. She improved quickly. (as a result of)
a: She improved quickly as a result of training every morning.

q: The students cheated on the exam. The school took strict action. (as a consequence of)
a: The school took strict action as a consequence of the students cheating on the exam.

q: The shop was closed. We couldn't buy groceries. (so)
a: The shop was closed, so we couldn't buy groceries.

q: He worked late. He missed dinner. (because)
a: He missed dinner because he worked late.

q: The team lost focus. They lost the match. (as a result)
a: The team lost focus; as a result, they lost the match.

q: The engine overheated. The driver stopped the car. (therefore)
a: The engine overheated; therefore, the driver stopped the car.

q: She saved money for months. She bought a new laptop. (so)
a: She saved money for months, so she bought a new laptop.

q: I didn't water the plants. They dried up. (because)
a: The plants dried up because I didn't water them.

q: The child cried loudly. He dropped his ice cream. (as a result of)
a: The child cried loudly as a result of dropping his ice cream.

q: The lights weren't working. The technician was called. (due to)
a: The technician was called due to the lights not working.

q: He forgot the meeting. We had to reschedule. (as a consequence)
a: He forgot the meeting; as a consequence, we had to reschedule.

q: The room was noisy. She couldn't concentrate. (since)
a: Since the room was noisy, she couldn't concentrate.

q: He followed all the rules. He avoided penalties. (so)
a: He followed all the rules, so he avoided penalties.

q: We were careful. There were no injuries. (as a result of)
a: There were no injuries as a result of our being careful.
